name: Camino Francés: Hospital de Órbigo - Villavante Loop
name_original: Hospital de Órbigo y Villavante — circular por el Camino Francés
description: Hike the moderate 7.7-mile Camino Francés: Hospital de Órbigo - Villavante Loop, featuring the historic Passo Honroso Bridge.
region_intro: Hiking around Hospital De Órbigo offers routes through the flat plains of the Órbigo River basin, characterized by agricultural landscapes and historical paths. The region features gentle elevation changes, with most trails following the course of the river or connecting small villages. Some routes venture into slightly hillier terrain, providing varied perspectives of the surrounding countryside. Best hiking trails around Hospital De Órbigo The most popular hiking route is Camino Francés: Hospital de Órbigo Alternative Route, a 6.9 miles (11.1 km) trail that takes about 2 hours 50 minutes to complete. This moderate route follows a section of the historic Camino Francés. Another top favourite among local hikers is Camino Francés: Hospital de Órbigo - Villavante Loop, a moderate 7.7 miles (12.4 km) path. This loop trail explores the rural landscape between Hospital de Órbigo and Villavante. Local hikers also love the Santo Toribio Cross Viewpoint – The Garden of the Soul loop from Santibáñez de Valdeiglesias, a 14.8 miles (23.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain with a viewpoint, often completed in about 6 hours 20 minutes. Hiking around Hospital De Órbigo is defined by river paths, historical pilgrimage routes, and agricultural plains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging long-distance routes. The routes in Hospital De Órbigo are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Hospital De Órbigo's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are there in Hospital De Órbigo?
A: There are 14 hiking routes around Hospital De Órbigo, offering a variety of experiences from easy strolls along the river to more challenging long-distance paths.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Hospital De Órbigo?
A: Yes, Hospital De Órbigo offers 2 easy hiking routes. These trails typically follow the flat plains of the Órbigo River basin, making them suitable for those new to hiking or looking for a relaxed stroll.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Hospital De Órbigo?
A: The terrain around Hospital De Órbigo is characterized by the flat plains of the Órbigo River basin, agricultural landscapes, and historical paths. Most trails feature gentle elevation changes, often following the river or connecting small villages. Some routes venture into slightly hillier terrain, providing varied perspectives.

Q: Are there any circular walks available in Hospital De Órbigo?
A: Yes, there are several circular walks. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1169394172/camino-frances-hospital-de-orbigo-villavante-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Francés: Hospital de Órbigo - Villavante Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 7.7-mile (12.4 km) path that explores the rural landscape. For a longer, more challenging loop, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38281593" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santo Toribio Cross Viewpoint – The Garden of the Soul loop from Santibáñez de Valdeiglesias&lt;/a&gt;, which is 14.8 miles (23.9 km) long.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or attractions to see while hiking in the area?
A: Many routes pass by historical and cultural points of interest. You can see the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/395479" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Passo Honroso Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, a medieval bridge famous for a historical joust. Other notable sights include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1000796" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Garden of the Soul&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4818580" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tengo Sed Pilgrim Statue&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4816502" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of St John the Baptist&lt;/a&gt; in Hospital de Órbigo itself.

Q: What is the typical duration for hikes around Hospital De Órbigo?
A: Hike durations vary significantly. Shorter, easy routes can take around 2-3 hours, while moderate trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1169374948/camino-frances-hospital-de-orbigo-alternative-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Francés: Hospital de Órbigo Alternative Route&lt;/a&gt; typically take about 2 hours 50 minutes. Longer, more difficult routes can extend to 6 hours or more, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38282255" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge over the Órbigo River – Passo Honroso Bridge loop from San Martín del Camino&lt;/a&gt;, which takes around 5 hours 25 minutes.

Q: Are the hiking trails in Hospital De Órbigo suitable for families?
A: Yes, many of the trails are suitable for families, especially the easier routes that follow the riverbanks and pass through villages. These paths generally have gentle gradients and offer pleasant scenery without being overly strenuous. Always check the specific route details for distance and difficulty to ensure it matches your family's capabilities.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Hospital De Órbigo?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the peaceful atmosphere, the historical significance of the Camino Francés sections, and the pleasant riverine and agricultural landscapes that define the region.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads in Hospital De Órbigo?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in and around Hospital De Órbigo, particularly in the villages where many routes begin or pass through. It's advisable to check specific starting points for designated parking areas or street parking availability.

Q: Can I find a hike that includes a scenic viewpoint?
A: Yes, for a hike that includes a scenic viewpoint, consider the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38281593" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santo Toribio Cross Viewpoint – The Garden of the Soul loop from Santibáñez de Valdeiglesias&lt;/a&gt;. This route leads through varied terrain and offers rewarding views from the Santo Toribio Cross.

Q: Are there any moderate difficulty hikes that are not part of the Camino Francés?
A: Yes, while many routes are associated with the Camino Francés, you can find other moderate options. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38281818" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albergue San Miguel Art Hostel – Passo Honroso Bridge loop from Villares de Órbigo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.2-mile (10 km) route that offers a different perspective of the local landscape.
tips:
Saint John the Baptist (San Juan Bautista), to whom this church in Hospital de Orbigo is dedicated, is – like Saint Joseph – a man on the threshold between the Old and the New Testament. His parents are Zechariah and Elisabeth. After the proclamation of God's incarnation, the Mother of God visits her relative Elisabeth, who will give birth to a son in old age, John the Baptist.
This is half a year older than Jesus and is considered his forerunner / pioneer. He lives in the desert, preaches the repentance of sins to the Jewish population and baptizes them in the Jordan, where Jesus also comes to be baptized, being filled with the Holy Spirit that descends on him from heaven.
The memorial day of John the Baptist (San Juan Bautista) in the calendar of saints is on June 24th, exactly half a year before 'Christmas Eve'.

The bridge was the point chosen by the knight of León Suero de Quiñones to challenge as many knights to a duel for a month between July 10 and August 9, 1434. The tournament, known as the Paso de Honor, today gives name to the bridge and to the medieval fair that is celebrated in Hospital de Órbigo the first weekend of June. &gt; The bridge was the point chosen by the knight of León, Suero de Quiñones, to challenge as many knights to a duel for a month. The tournament took place between July 10 and August 9, 1434 and was called "Paso de Honor". Today it gives its name to the bridge and to the medieval fair that is held in Hospital de Órbigo on the first weekend of June.
Here we see the Apostle James the Elder, himself on the road as a pilgrim.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Camino Francés: Hospital de Órbigo - Villavante Loop?
faqs.answer: This loop typically takes around 3 hours and 10 minutes to complete. The total distance is approximately 12.4 kilometers (7.7 miles).

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is generally flat and straightforward, characteristic of the 'Meseta-style' landscape. You'll encounter agricultural fields, some oak groves, and sections that run alongside roads. While some parts might be less picturesque due to proximity to roads, alternative paths can offer a quieter experience through the countryside.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The most notable feature is the historic Passo Honroso Bridge in Hospital de Órbigo, a medieval stone bridge with 19 arches famous for a 15th-century jousting tournament. You'll also pass the Church of St John the Baptist in Hospital de Órbigo and the Monument to the Pilgrim of Villavante.

faqs.question: Is the Camino Francés: Hospital de Órbigo - Villavante Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, while rated as 'moderate' due to its length, the trail is generally flat with minimal elevation gain, making it accessible for beginners who are comfortable with walking for a few hours. The primary challenge is the distance rather than technical difficulty.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Camino Francés is generally dog-friendly. However, always ensure your dog is on a leash, especially when near roads or agricultural areas, and be mindful of other pilgrims and local wildlife. Carry enough water for both yourself and your dog.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: Spring (April-May) and Autumn (September-October) are generally the best times. The weather is milder, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter. These seasons also offer pleasant scenery with blooming fields or autumn colors.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the trailhead, and is there public transport access?
faqs.answer: Parking is typically available in Hospital de Órbigo, particularly near the historic bridge or in the village center. For public transport, Hospital de Órbigo is a common stop on bus routes serving the Camino Francés, connecting it to larger towns like León. Check local bus schedules for the most up-to-date information.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay any entrance fees to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to hike this section of the Camino Francés. It is a public route accessible to all.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route is part of the larger Camino Francés, specifically intersecting with sections of the official "Camino Francés - 06 León a Cacabelos" and "Camiño Francés (Rutas alternativas)". You'll also find segments of "Carretera vieja - Camino de Santiago" and "Calle Santa Marina".

faqs.question: Are there any alternative routes if I prefer a more scenic experience?
faqs.answer: Yes, while the main Camino route can involve walking alongside roads, there are often alternative, more rural paths that diverge through fields and oak groves. These can offer a quieter, more natural experience, though they might add a few kilometers to your total distance. Look for signs indicating 'rutas alternativas' or consult a detailed map.

faqs.question: Are there amenities like cafes or restrooms available on the route?
faqs.answer: Hospital de Órbigo offers various amenities, including cafes, restaurants, and restrooms. Villavante, being a smaller village, provides basic refreshments for pilgrims. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially between villages.
